mechanism_narrative: A low and stable federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy, which reduces the cost of capital and supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals an economic downturn, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the medium term.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust housing starts indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand, suggesting a healthy economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND HOUST > 1000 (thousands of units)
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a period of low inflation signals efficient supply-side growth, leading to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the longer term.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances within the specified low inflation regime.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth indicates a strong underlying economy, which translates into higher corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, reflects a healthy labor market and strong consumer spending potential, which is a positive driver for corporate revenues and S&P 500 returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months following PAYEMS growth above 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid acceleration in consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power and can lead to tighter monetary policy, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL growth > 0.4% MoM for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer and business confidence, indicating a potential slowdown in economic activity that would negatively impact corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over the medium term.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 10% MoM for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods following two consecutive months of >10% decline in HOUST.
